channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: North Korea displays new long-range missile at a military parade celebrating Kim Jong Un
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — At a massive military parade attended by foreign leaders, North Korean leader Kim Jong Un rolled out his nuclear-armed military’s most powerful weapons, including a new intercontinental ballistic missile he may be preparing to test in coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Two powerful quakes strike off southern Philippines, killing at least 7 people
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Two powerful offshore earthquakes struck the same region in the southern Philippines hours apart on Friday with the first 7.4 magnitude temblor killing at least seven people, setting off landslides and prompting evacuations of coastal areas nearby because of a brief tsunam
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Power restored to 800,000 in Kyiv after major Russian strikes on Ukraine's energy grid
item.description: Power has been restored to over 800,000 residents in Kyiv, a day after Russia launched major attacks on Ukraine's power grid, causing widespread blackouts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Kim Jong Un shows off 'most powerful' ballistic missile as foreign leaders watch North Korea military parade
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un unveiled the untested Hwasong-20 ICBM at a Worker's Party 80th anniversary parade, Friday, calling it the most powerful nuclear strategic weapons system.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thousands of Palestinians return to what's left of their homes as Gaza ceasefire takes effect
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — Tens of thousands of Palestinians headed back to the heavily destroyed northern Gaza Strip on Friday as a U.S.-brokered ceasefire came into effect in a deal that raised hopes for ending the Israel-Hamas war. All the remaining hostages were set to be released within days.
item.dc:creator: ABDEL KAREEM HANA, WAFAA SHURAFA and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado wins the Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: OSLO, Norway (AP) — Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado won the Nobel Peace Prize on Friday for her struggle to achieve a democratic transition in the South American nation, winning recognition as a woman “who keeps the flame of democracy burning amid a growing darkness.
item.dc:creator: KOSTYA MANENKOV, REGINA GARCIA CANO and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Putin praises Trump's peace efforts despite failing to win the Nobel prize
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Friday praised U.S. President Donald Trump's peace efforts, despite not winning a Nobel, and voiced hope for securing an agreement to extend a pivotal nuclear arms pact with Washington for another year.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The Taliban government blames Pakistan for a strike on Kabul
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — The Taliban government on Friday blamed Pakistan for striking the Afghan capital and bombing a market in the country's east, accusing its neighbor of violating national airspace. A blast struck before 10 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The Latest: Tens of thousands of Palestinians in Gaza return north as ceasefire takes effect
item.description: Tens of thousands of Palestinians are starting to walk north to return to their homes after the Israeli military said the ceasefire agreement with Hamas came into effect at noon local time and that troops were withdrawing to the agreed-upon deployment lines.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: India to upgrade Kabul mission to full embassy, signaling deeper ties with Taliban
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — India is upgrading its technical mission in Kabul to a full embassy, India’s foreign minister announced Friday after meeting his Afghanistan counterpart in New Delhi.
item.dc:creator: AIJAZ HUSSAIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taiwan's president pledges to build air defense system in face of China threat
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan will accelerate the building of a “Taiwan Shield” or “T-Dome” air defense system in the face of the military threat from China, its leader said Friday. President Lai Ching-te also pledged to raise defense spending to more than 3% of GDP and to reach 5% by 2030.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU and SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: King Mohammed VI set to speak as Gen Z protesters demand reforms in Morocco
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — Morocco's elusive King Mohammed VI inaugurates parliament Friday in a speech with far higher stakes than in recent years, as anti-government protests sweep the nation's cities, questioning the government's spending priorities.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean investigators indict Unification Church leader over alleged ties to ex-first lady
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean investigators on Friday indicted the 82-year-old leader of the Unification Church, Hak Ja Han, over allegations that she instructed church officials to bribe the wife of jailed former President Yoon Suk Yeol and a conservative lawmaker close to him.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Peru’s Congress removes President Boluarte as a crime wave grips the country
item.description: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Peru’s Congress voted early Friday to remove deeply unpopular President Dina Boluarte from office as a crime wave grips the South American nation and quickly replaced her with 38-year-old lawyer José Jerí, the legislative body's leader.
item.dc:creator: FRANKLIN BRICEÑO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France's Macron asks newly resigned Prime Minister Lecornu to try again to form a government
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron on Friday re-appointed Sebastien Lecornu as prime minister, just days after his resignation, to try again to form a government in a bid to end the country’s political deadlock.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Madagascar's Gen Z protesters clash with police as they call for president to step down
item.description: ANTANANARIVO, Madagascar (AP) — About 1,000 protesters marched in Madagascar's capital on Thursday, clashing with police who used tear gas and stun grenades to disperse them in the third week of what has been the most significant unrest in years in the Indian Ocean island nation.
item.dc:creator: SARAH TÉTAUD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Nobel Peace Prize nominations
item.description: Throughout his second term, President Trump has campaigned for the Nobel Peace Prize. The president, his allies and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu have renewed the push since Israel and Hamas agreed to the first phase of a peace plan Trump proposed last month.
item.dc:creator: Max Rego, The Hill

item.title: Exclusive: Israeli ambassador says no peace in Gaza unless Hamas hands over all 48 hostages, disarms
item.description: Israel's Ambassador Yechiel Leiter warns the Gaza war will continue unless Hamas returns all hostages and completely disarms under the new peace agreement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: From grief to gratitude: Trump praised as Israelis celebrate Hamas hostage deal in Tel Aviv
item.description: Israelis express joy, relief and grief at Hostages Square following President Donald Trump's announcement of agreement to end Israel-Hamas war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine's new missiles and drones cause gas shortages in Russia, Zelenskyy says
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Strikes on Russian oil facilities by Ukraine’s newly developed long-range missiles and drones are causing significant gas shortages in Russia, while on the battlefield a recent Ukrainian counter-offensive has derailed Russia’s plans to capture parts of the eastern Donetsk region
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US diplomat fired over relationship with woman accused of Chinese Communist Party ties
item.description: The dismissal is believed to be the first of its kind for violating a ban on such relationships that was introduced late last year under the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas accepts Trump peace plan ending 2 years of war in Gaza, returning hostages
item.description: Hamas agrees to President Donald Trump's Gaza peace plan that calls for release of all 48 hostages and complete Israeli withdrawal in exchange for Hamas disarmament.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Anti-Hamas Gaza militias reject terror group, declare support for Trump’s peace plan
item.description: Support for a peace plan grows as negotiators work in Cairo to seal the deal, with Gazan militias backing Trump’s proposal amid fighting and calls for new local governance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Before and after images show devastating destruction in Gaza
item.description: Satellite imagery reveals widespread devastation in Gaza cities, including Rafah and Beit Hanoun, since the start of the Israel-Hamas war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian strike seriously damages Ukrainian power plant as winter approaches, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia seriously damaged one of Ukraine’s thermal power plants in an overnight attack, authorities said Wednesday, as Moscow pursued its campaign to deny Ukrainians heat, light and running water as winter approaches.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli military intercepts another flotilla heading to Gaza, detains scores of activists
item.description: The ministry said the 145 activists, who were in good health, were being brought to shore in Israel for processing and are expected to be deported soon.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and RENATA BRITO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Hamas Oct 7 attacks survivor recalls Gaza captivity 2 years later
item.description: Two years after the Oct. 7 Hamas attack on Israel, survivor Aviva Siegel recounts the torture and starvation she endured during her Gaza captivity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Newly elected German mayor fights for life after stabbing attack
item.description: German mayor-elect Iris Stalzer was hospitalized in critical condition following a stabbing attack at her home in the German town of Herdecke.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel and Hamas launch indirect talks in Egypt on eve of Gaza war anniversary
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israeli and Hamas officials launched indirect talks Monday at an Egyptian resort on a U.S.-drafted peace plan to end the ruinous war in Gaza on the eve of its second anniversary. The talks, which went on for several hours, unfolded amid many questions about the plan presented by U.S.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, MELANIE LIDMAN and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Big Time Rush's James Maslow releases new song celebrating Israel
item.description: Maslow acknowledged he chose Oct. 7th because of how the "country (Israel) and this world have normalized a hate towards each other.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Israeli and Hamas officials discuss US peace plan for Gaza
item.description: Israeli and Hamas officials are holding indirect talks in an Egyptian resort Monday on a U.S. peace plan for Gaza.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Japan’s ruling party elects Sanae Takaichi as new leader, likely to become first female PM
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan’s governing party on Saturday elected former Economic Security Minister Sanae Takaichi, a hard-line ultra-conservative and China hawk, as its new leader, making her likely to become the country’s first female prime minister.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, NewsNation
